phonation.name: Sustained phonation
phonation.description: The participant sustains a single vowel sound (e.g., /a/, /e/, /i/, /o/, /u/) to analyze voice stability and control. Recording uses *delayed Stop mode* — the Stop button becomes available only after the set duration expires. The administrator should set the vowel to be produced ('phoneme'), the number of repetitions ('repeat'), and the time interval after which the Stop button appears ('duration').
phonation.title: Prolonged phonation /{{phoneme}}/
phonation.instructions: Draw a deep breath, and say /{{phoneme}}/ as steadily and for as long as possible in one breath. {{example}} Try to sustain it for at least {{minDuration}} seconds. Press Start to begin and Stop once you run out of breath.

syllableRepeating.name: Syllable repetition
syllableRepeating.description: The participant repeatedly produces given syllables (e.g., 'pa-ta-ka', 'ta', 'ka') as fast and clearly as possible to assess articulation speed and precision. Recording uses *countdown mode* — it automatically stops after the specified duration. The administrator should define the syllable sequence ('syllable'), total recording time ('duration'), and the number of repetitions ('repeat') if applicable.
syllableRepeating.title: {{syllable}} repetition
syllableRepeating.instructions:
Draw a deep breath and repeat the syllables
/{{syllable}}-{{syllable}}/
as quickly and accurately as possible until the task finishes automatically after {{duration}} seconds. {{example}} Press Start to begin.

retelling.name: Fairytale retelling
retelling.description: The participant retells a known fairytale from memory (e.g., Snow White, Hansel and Gretel) to evaluate spontaneous connected speech, fluency, and lexical richness. Recording uses *manual stop mode* — the administrator or participant manually stops the recording when finished. The administrator should select the fairytale ('fairytale') and the number of repetitions ('repeat').
retelling.title: Retelling fairytale
retelling.instructions:
Press Play story to listen to a spoken fairytale. {{playStory}} After listening to the story, press Start and retell the tale in your own words in {{duration}} seconds.

Speak continuously, so it doesn't matter if the retold tale is correct or complete.
retelling.instructionsActive: Retell the fairytale in your own words.

reading.name: Reading text
reading.description: The participant reads a short text displayed on the screen aloud to assess reading fluency, articulation, and prosody. Recording uses *manual stop mode* — it starts when START is pressed and stops when the participant or administrator presses STOP. The administrator should choose the text topic ('topic') and number of repetitions ('repeat').
reading.title: Reading {{topic}}
reading.instructions:
Press Start and read the text on the screen out loud.

You might need to scroll down to reach the end.

Press Stop when you are finished.

northWind.label: The North Wind and the Sun
northWind.text: The North Wind and the Sun were disputing which was the stronger, when a traveller, came along wrapped in a warm cloak. They agreed that the one who first succeeded in, making the traveller take his cloak off should be considered stronger than the other. Then the North Wind blew as hard as he could, but the more he blew the more closely did the traveller fold his cloak around him, and at last the North Wind gave up the attempt. Then the Sun shone out warmly, and immediately the traveller took off his cloak. And so the North Wind was obliged to confess that the Sun was the stronger of the two.

grandfather.label: The Grandfather
grandfather.text: You wished to know all about my grandfather. Well, he is nearly ninety-three years old; he dresses himself in an ancient black frock coat, usually minus several buttons; yet he still thinks as swiftly as ever. A long, flowing beard clings to his chin, giving those who observe him a pronounced feeling of the utmost respect. When he speaks, his voice is just a bit cracked and quivers a trifle. Twice each day he plays skilfully and with zest upon our small organ. Except in the winter when the ooze or snow or ice prevents, he slowly takes a short walk in the open air each day. We have often urged him to walk more and smoke less, but he always answers, “Banana oil!” Grandfather likes to be modern in his language.

rainbow.label: The Rainbow
rainbow.text: When the sunlight strikes raindrops in the air, they act as a prism and form a rainbow. The rainbow is a division of white light into many beautiful colours. These take the shape of a long round arch, with its path high above, and its two ends apparently beyond the horizon. There is , according to legend, a boiling pot of gold at one end. People look, but no one ever finds it. When a man looks for something beyond his reach, his friends say he is looking for the pot of gold at the end of the rainbow.Throughout the centuries people have explained the rainbow in various ways. Some have accepted it as a miracle without physical explanation. To the Hebrews it was a token that there would be no more universal floods. The Greeks used to imagine that it was a sign from the gods to foretell war or heavy rain. The Norsemen considered the rainbow as a bridge over which the gods passed from earth to their home in the sky. Others have tried to explain the phenomenon physically. Aristotle thought that the rainbow was caused by reflection of the sun's rays by the rain. Since then physicists have found that it is not reflection, but refraction by the raindrops which causes the rainbows. Many complicated ideas about the rainbow have been formed. The difference in the rainbow depends considerably upon the size of the drops, and the width of the coloured band increases as the size of the drops increases. The actual primary rainbow observed is said to be the effect of super-imposition of a number of bows. If the red of the second bow falls upon the green of the first, the result is to give a bow with an abnormally wide yellow band, since red and green light when mixed form yellow. This is a very common type of bow, one showing mainly red and yellow, with little or no green or blue.

monologue.name: Monologue on topic
monologue.description: The participant talks freely about a given topic (e.g., hobbies, family, or any chosen subject) to assess speech fluency and coherence. Recording uses *delayed stop mode* — the STOP button appears only after the set time interval has passed. The administrator should define the topic ('topic'), the number of repetitions ('repeat'), and the duration after which the STOP button becomes available ('duration').
monologue.title: Monologue

dayDescription.label: Day description
dayDescription.topicDescription: Please try to describe in as much detail as possible how your yesterday, past week, or past month went. Try to include details, such as what you had for breakfast, what interesting things you experienced, or who you met. The ideal length is two minutes. When you are ready, press the Start button and begin speaking.

dynamic_monologue.name: Monologue
dynamic_monologue.description: The participant talks freely about a given topic (e.g., hobbies, family, or any chosen subject) to assess speech fluency and coherence. Recording uses *delayed stop mode* — the STOP button appears only after the set time interval has passed. The administrator should define the topic ('topic'), the number of repetitions ('repeat'), and the duration after which the STOP button becomes available ('duration').
dynamic_monologue.title: Monologue
dynamic_monologue.instructions:
Please speak about the topic below for a total of {{duration}} seconds. If you run out of things to say, an option to change the topic will appear to help you continue.

Topic:
{{topicDescription}}

Press Start when you are ready.

everyday.label: Daily Routine
everyday.topicDescription: What does a typical day look like for you from the moment you wake up? You might include what your morning routine is like and what you usually do later in the day.

entertainment.label: Movies, Books, and Media
entertainment.topicDescription: What is your favourite movie, book, or TV show? You can describe what it is about, and whether you would recommend it to others.

hobbies.label: Free Time & Interests
hobbies.topicDescription: How do you like to spend your free time? You can describe an activity you enjoy, how you got into it, and what makes it interesting for you.

eating.label: Food & Cooking
eating.topicDescription: Do you prefer cooking at home or eating out? You can describe meals you enjoy or a restaurant you like to visit.

typicalWeekend.label: Typical Weekend
typicalWeekend.topicDescription: What does a normal weekend look like for you? You can describe what you usually do on Saturdays or Sundays, and how do these days differ from your work week?

work.label: Work
work.topicDescription: What kind of work do you do, or have you done in the past? You might describe your profession and what your typical daily tasks look or looked like.

travel.label: Travel and Transport
travel.topicDescription: How do you usually get from place to place? You can describe your usual commute or a favorite way of travelling and talk about a trip or a place you have found interesting.

neighborhood.label: Neighborhood
neighborhood.topicDescription: What is the place and neighborhood where you live like? You can describe its characteristics, and some places which you visit often.

shopping.label: Grocery Shopping
shopping.topicDescription: How do you usually go grocery shopping? You can describe the process from making a list to moving through the store, and how you feel about shopping in-person versus online.

d15colour.name: D-15 Colour Test
d15colour.description: A colour vision test where the participant arranges 15 coloured caps in a sequence. Includes Saturated (Farnsworth) and Desaturated (Lanthony) versions.
d15colour.title: Colour Arrangement Task
d15colour.instructions:
1. Look at the last colour in the top row.
2. Tap the colour from the pool below that most closely matches it.
3. Repeat until the top row is full to unlock the Submit button.

Swipe the top row left or right to see all your placed colours.
d15colour.addText:
• Tap a colour in the bottom pool.
• It's added to the next empty space in the top row.
d15colour.goalText: Your goal: create smooth, gradual sequence in the top row.
d15colour.modifyText:
• Tap a colour in the top row.
• It's removed, so you can pick a different colour instead.
d15colour.disclaimerText: If you have trouble telling colours apart, that's OK — just choose what looks closest to you.
d15colour.demoText:
Goal: Form a smooth, continuous sequence of colours.

• Add a cap: Tap a colour in the bottom pool. It will appear in the first empty space in the top row.
• Remove a cap: Tap it in the top row.
• Start over: Use the Reset button.
d15colour.demoInstructions:
How to add colour: Tap a colour in the bottom pool. It will appear in the first empty space in the top row.

How to modify sequence: Tap
d15colour.trialCompleteText: Trial completed. Now the test will be more challenging.
d15colour.timeoutText: It's okay to stop here. Would you like to submit your current sequence?

preTestChecklist.title: Vision Task Setup
preTestChecklist.subtitle: Please complete the following steps. Tap the info icon for help. Mark your status for each step.

landscape.label: Rotate your phone sideways.
landscape.helpText:
If the screen doesn't rotate with your phone, you need to unlock it.

Open your phone's quick control menu (swipe down from the top edge of your screen, or up from the bottom on older iPhones).

Look for the rotation icon ( ) and tap it so your screen is allowed to rotate.

brightness.label: Turn brightness all the way up.
brightness.helpText:
Open your phone's quick control menu (swipe down from the top edge of your screen, or up from the bottom on older iPhones).

Find the brightness slider (usually a sun icon ) and drag it to the absolute maximum.

Note: If your phone turns the brightness back down on its own, look for an Auto or Adaptive button nearby, or look in your phone's Settings > Display and turn it off.

Example:

colourFilters.label: Turn off screen colour filters (yellow light).
colourFilters.helpText:
Filters alter your screen's natural colours, often making it look yellow or warm.

How to turn them off:
1. Open Settings .
2. Tap or search for Display.
3. Turn off True Tone, Night Shift, Eye Comfort Shield, or Reading Mode, or any other mode that adjusts screen colours.

privacyScreen.label: Do you have a privacy protection glass?
privacyScreen.helpText:
It is a special glass cover that stops people next to you from reading your screen.

How to check: Tilt your phone to the side. If the screen suddenly looks completely dark or black, you have a privacy protector on your phone.

environment.label: Sit indoors in normal room lighting.
environment.helpText: Avoid direct sunlight or bright windows shining onto your screen.

rotateBack.title: Vision Test Complete
rotateBack.message: You may now rotate your phone back to continue.

sdmt.name: Symbol Digit Modalities Test
sdmt.description: A cognitive test where the participant matches symbols to numbers based on a reference key. The test measures processing speed, attention, and visual-motor coordination. Recording uses *basic stop mode* — it starts when START is pressed and stops when the participant or administrator presses STOP. The administrator should set the test duration ('duration') and whether to show an on-screen keypad for responses ('showKeypad').
sdmt.title: Symbols
sdmt.instructions:
A symbol will appear in the center. Using the key, tap the matching number with one finger.

Work as fast as you can, without making mistakes. You have {{duration}} seconds.
sdmt.instructionsActive: Match the symbol to the number.
sdmt.completedInstructions:
Symbols task completed successfully!
Click Next to proceed, or Try Again if you want to repeat the task.
sdmt.demoText: Match the symbol to the corresponding number using the keypad.
sdmt.resultsSaved: Your results have been saved securely.
